summary: Test jruby on evergreen - driver
description: Currently jruby is only tested on Travis. During the day Travis backlog can be over 30 minutes whereas evergreen builds complete much quicker.

summary: NoServerAvailable message should include seed list and current server addresses
description:
Currently NoServerAvailable exception message includes various details of the server selector (besides primary) but doesn't include any information about either the seeds or the current cluster. This generally makes it difficult to identify *why* the driver could not find a server, because any of the following may have taken place:

1. Driver failed to connect to any of the servers, for example because the hostname/IP was misspelled.
2. Driver failed to connect to any of the servers because it tried to connecting to a temporary development port and that mongod instance was shut down.
3. Driver failed to select a server because it is trying to connect to a cluster with no server addresses in it.
4. Driver failed to select a server because the cluster makeup changed at some point in the past and then the cluster was reset to its original configuration, but driver keeps trying to connect to the changed configuration.

Many of these cases would be immediately obvious if only the exception message stated what the seed list was and what the current cluster server addresses are.

Note that as far as I know we don't currently save the seed list, thus part of the work in this ticket is to save the seed list in the client somewhere. Possibly even the SRV address.

summary: Set up junit xml-ish test reports in evergreen
description: See: https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-python-driver/blob/3.7.1/.evergreen/config.yml#L341-L347

summary: Add ruby 2.2 to toolchain
Resync SDAM tests
description: Added some mixed case hostnames into the SDAM test corpus.

summary: Test replica sets and sharded clusters on Travis
description: Currently Travis only tests a standalone mongod, as a result when Travis tests run quicker than evergreen tests (late in the evening) there's no feedback on whether the RS/SC tests are succeeding.

summary: max_staleness does not work due to seconds / milliseconds mismatch
description:
The max_staleness read preference is specified in seconds. However, during the server selection check, there is a mismatch when comparing the staleness: The actual staleness is calculated in seconds, but compared to the configured preference converted to milliseconds (see below). This means that if e.g. the max_staleness is set to 90, it will only ignore servers that are 25 hours stale (90 * 1000 / 3600 == 25).

 

The code is here:

[https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-ruby-driver/blob/v2.5.3/lib/mongo/server_selector/selectable.rb#L229]

Copied, with my comments:
{code:ruby}      def filter_stale_servers(candidates, primary = nil)
        return candidates unless @max_staleness
        max_staleness_ms = @max_staleness * 1000

        if primary
          candidates.select do |server|
            validate_max_staleness_support!(server)
            # last_scan and last_write_date are Time objects. Subtracting them gives a duration in seconds (Float)
            staleness = (server.last_scan - server.last_write_date) - # seconds
                        (primary.last_scan - primary.last_write_date)  + # seconds
                        (server.heartbeat_frequency_seconds * 1000) # ms
            staleness <= max_staleness_ms
          end
        else
          max_write_date = candidates.collect(&:last_write_date).max
          candidates.select do |server|
            validate_max_staleness_support!(server)
            # Same issue here: max_write_date and last_write_date are Time objects
            staleness = max_write_date - server.last_write_date + (server.heartbeat_frequency_seconds * 1000)
            staleness <= max_staleness_ms
          end
        end
      end
{code}
 
Note that there are tests in the code, but the types are different in the tests:

[https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-ruby-driver/blob/v2.5.3/spec/mongo/max_staleness_spec.rb#L61]

In the tests, the values are numbers in ms, instead of Time objects as in reality.

summary: Seed not removed from server list during Client construction when it is not in replica set config
description:
I created a cluster with 3 nodes at [localhost:27017, localhost:27018, localhost:27019]. Ismaster output:

{code}
replset:SECONDARY> rs.isMaster()
{
	"hosts" : [
		"localhost:27017",
		"localhost:27018",
		"localhost:27019"
	],
	"setName" : "replset",
	"setVersion" : 1,
	"ismaster" : false,
	"secondary" : true,
	"primary" : "localhost:27019",
	"me" : "localhost:27017",
	"lastWrite" : {
		"opTime" : {
			"ts" : Timestamp(1528732740, 1),
			"t" : NumberLong(3)
		},
		"lastWriteDate" : ISODate("2018-06-11T15:59:00Z"),
		"majorityOpTime" : {
			"ts" : Timestamp(1528732740, 1),
			"t" : NumberLong(3)
		},
		"majorityWriteDate" : ISODate("2018-06-11T15:59:00Z")
	},
	"maxBsonObjectSize" : 16777216,
	"maxMessageSizeBytes" : 48000000,
	"maxWriteBatchSize" : 100000,
	"localTime" : ISODate("2018-06-11T15:59:02.959Z"),
	"logicalSessionTimeoutMinutes" : 30,
	"minWireVersion" : 0,
	"maxWireVersion" : 6,
	"readOnly" : false,
	"ok" : 1,
	"operationTime" : Timestamp(1528732740, 1),
	"$clusterTime" : {
		"clusterTime" : Timestamp(1528732740, 1),
		"signature" : {
			"hash" : BinData(0,"AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA="),
			"keyId" : NumberLong(0)
		}
	}
}
{code}

Then I requested to connect to the following URI: 

MONGODB_URI = 'mongodb://127.0.0.1:27017'

Ruby driver settled on the following cluster:

{code}
#<Mongo::Client:0x46957295449420 cluster=#<Mongo::Cluster:0x46957295427420
  addresses=["127.0.0.1:27017", "localhost:27017", "localhost:27018", "localhost:27019"]
  topology=Replica Set
  servers=["#<Mongo::Server:0x46957295069720 address=localhost:27019 PRIMARY replica_set=replset>", "#<Mongo::Server:0x46957295330800 address=localhost:27018 SECONDARY replica_set=replset>", "#<Mongo::Server:0x46957295280400 address=localhost:27017 SECONDARY replica_set=replset>", "#<Mongo::Server:0x46957295423120 address=127.0.0.1:27017 SECONDARY replica_set=replset>"]
{code}

27017 appears twice.

Note the following language in https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/server-discovery-and-monitoring/server-discovery-and-monitoring.rst:

> If a hidden member is provided as a seed, the client can use it to find the primary. Since the hidden member does not appear in the primary's host list, it will be removed once the primary is checked.

...

> The client strives to fill the "servers" list only with servers that the primary said were members of the replica set, when the client most recently contacted the primary.

This suggests that 127.0.0.1:27017 should be removed from the cluster once the primary is discovered.

Python client settles on the following server list which appears to be in line with the spec:

frozenset([(u'localhost', 27019), (u'localhost', 27017), (u'localhost', 27018)])

Interestingly enough, if initial connection to the seed fails, and seed comes up later, once the seed is contacted the Ruby driver correctly removes the seed from the address list (https://github.com/p-mongo/tests/blob/master/connect/connect_late.rb). It appears that the address list is dependent on whether the servers are discovered during client construction or later.

The tests are in https://github.com/p-mongo/tests/tree/master/connect.

summary: Separate ReplicaSetNoPrimary & ReplicaSetWithPrimary
description:
SDAM spec calls for these two topology types to be separate. Ruby driver currently only has a ReplicaSet topology which includes both of these. Part of the reason is perhaps because Ruby driver tracks topology as statically configured only and does not track its dynamic state (e.g. whether it has a primary) - that's done in server selection objects.

[~behackett] Thoughts on this?

summary: OperationError#code not set on duplicate key error
description:
On certain kinds of errors like duplicate key errors, no code is set on the OperationError exception. 

Steps to reproduce:

{code:ruby}
require 'mongo'
client = Mongo::Client.new("mongodb://localhost/test")

begin
  client['test'].insert_one(_id: '1')
  client['test'].insert_one(_id: '1')
rescue => e
  puts "Code: #{e.code.inspect}"
end
{code}

Expected Result:

Prints "Code 11000"

Actual Result:

Prints "Code nil"

summary: Pass through unknown options in Options::Mapper
description:
Currently Options::Mapper silently discards unknown options. This makes the driver unable to support new server releases that add options that the current version of the driver does not know about.

Instead the driver should pass through options it does not recognize.

summary: Review and organize "administration" section of tutorial
description: The "administration" section seems to have a bunch of random stuff in it besides actual administration. For example it documents how to drop collections - this should probably be moved to collections section where it would be more easily discoverable. There's also a paragraph on read/write preferences which isn't exactly anything to do with administration.

summary: Make retry_writes a crud option
description:
Currently retry_writes option is considered a non-CRUD option in cluster parlance, which means when it's given to Client#with a new cluster in created. As far as I can see there is no reason to create a new cluster when retrying or not retrying writes, therefore this option should be added to the list of CRUD options.

Additionally, CRUD options should not be passed to cluster because a cluster is reused for multiple clients with different CRUD options.

Making these changes requires adjusting which clients some tests use due to ruby-1460.

summary: Separate test user creation from test suite
Change stream documentation exists in two places
description: There is some change stream documentation under "collections" in tutorial and change streams also have their own section. All of the documentation should probably be under the change streams section.

summary: Add tutorial documentation for change streams on databases and clusters
description: https://docs.mongodb.com/ruby-driver/master/tutorials/ruby-driver-change-streams/ does not say how to use change streams on databases and clusters, support for which was added in driver 2.6.

summary: Global registry of clients in test suite
description:
When we are testing SDAM, it is important to not have background threads from global clients and clients created by previously executing tests get in the way of the current test. This requires at a minimum suspending background monitoring of all outstanding clients. As there is utility in sharing client instances between tests, this ticket proposes to:

1. Add a global registry (glorified array) of mongo clients.
2. Replace all Mongo::Client.new calls with a helper invocation that adds the created client to the registry.
3. Add a helper method that kills background monitoring threads of all active clients. I believe calling `disconnect` would accomplish this.
4. Adds a helper method that reinstates background monitoring threads, such as by calling `reconnect`.

This also paves way for automatically cleaning up all clients created by individual test cases, if we mark the global clients specially.

summary: Implement SDAM monitoring heartbeat events
description:
The SDAM monitoring spec [defines the following heartbeat events|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/server-discovery-and-monitoring/server-discovery-and-monitoring-monitoring.rst#api]:

- ServerHeartbeatStartedEvent
- ServerHeartbeatSucceededEvent
- ServerHeartbeatFailedEvent

The driver doesn't appear to implement these events. The commits in RUBY-1096 make no mention of them.

Implement the missing events and document them with examples.

summary: Command monitoring example to log failing operations
description: For debugging RUBY-1397 we should have an example in the tutorial that sets up logging of failed commands/operations.

summary: Update mongo_kerberos gem
description: The gemspec needs to be updated to remove the upper bound on the Ruby driver version.

summary: Enable SNI on jruby where possible
Fix jruby test suite concurrent array modification

summary: Move AppMetadata to be under Server
description:
Currently AppMetadata exists under Cluster but there's no reason for this relationship. Furthermore AppMetadata pokes into cluster options which is also unnecessary.

- Move AppMetadata to be under Server, for consistency with our Connection classes
- Document options that its constructor takes
- Passing cluster's options (rather than client's options) to AppMetadata can be left alone for now

summary: Delete Mongo::Cluster::AppMetadata
description: This exists for BC only.

summary: Improve connection pool queue documentation and add invariants
description:
Connection pool queue is confusing to work with because half the methods refer to the connection pool rather than the queue, which is a queue of available connections only. The docs for each attribute/method should clearly state whether the attribute/method refers to the queue or the pool, where appropriate.

Additionally since we must leave some of the confusing methods in place for compatibility reasons, it will be good to have invariant checks using our lint framework to ensure the state is internally consistent. This becomes more important when we try to close connections upon enqueueing as per 1319.

summary: Run auth-nossl and noauth-ssl configurations in evergreen
description: Currently the tests we have either have auth and ssl off or both on. As a result, considerable time may be spent attempting to figure out an auth issue when in fact there is an SSL issue going on, or vice versa. Having auth-nossl and noauth-ssl configurations would have saved 2 days for 1319 by making it immediately obvious that the failure was SSL-related rather than auth-related.

summary: Handshake & auth exceptions may make connections permanently unusable
description: Due to @socket assignment early in the method, Connection#connect! makes the connection think it is connected even if the handshake and/or auth raise an exception. Subsequent calls to #socket and #connectable? both return true. Subsequent calls to #connect! are noops. The connection becomes unusable and will never recover by itself, though the driver thinks it is perfectly functional.

summary: Handshake changes for SDAM and Auth
description:
tl;dr:

- (/) Monitoring-only  sockets must not send SCRAM mechanism negotiation in {{isMaster}}
- (/) Monitoring-only  sockets must not authenticate at all.
- (/) Non-monitoring sockets (e.g. connection pool or single-threaded client) do a "normal" handshake and authenticate if there are credentials.
- (/) An authentication error on a socket must close all (and only) non-monitoring sockets to the same server.

Possible backward breaking change:
- (/) Some drivers were resetting a server's topology description to Unknown on an authentication error and should stop doing so.  This means the topology will always be "correct" even when authentication fails.
- (/) It will no longer be possible for authentication errors to be masked as server selection errors.

Detailed changes:
* https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/df4bc0b3224befda92af7a0bdfd10183deae29df
* https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/64ddab6b40e78aed2f7d2134195c04e382837eb3

summary: Close clients created in collections_spec
description: The test appears to be leaking client instances.

summary: Do not schedule zero cursors for finalization
description: Currently cursor constructor unconditionally schedules the cursor for being closed, even if the cursor is already exhausted by the time the constructor runs and cursor_id is zero. In the latter case there is no need to schedule the cursor for finalization.

summary: Bump wire protocol version for 4.2
description: See DRIVERS-571 for details.

summary: Test that killCursors really succeeds
description: See DRIVERS-430 for details.

summary: Change handling of network errors or timeouts during connection handshake
description:
See DRIVERS-429 for details.

https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/bfddbd98bf396104f78500f93f1f13e3fd2d8c3a

There is an event for server description transition from unknown to primary but there is no event for primary to unknown transition. A primary becoming unknown should also change topology description to no primary methinks.

summary: Update algorithm for Kerberos hostname canonicalization
Implement topology closed sdam event
description: We have the event defined but as far as I can tell do not ever publish it. We are also missing the log subscriber for it.

summary: Add SDAM monitoring example to ruby driver tutorial
description:
Ruby driver tutorial should have an example code & output that:

1. Sets up SDAM listener
2. Connects to cluster (SDAM events, cluster & topology state changes being logged)
3. Steps down the primary (SDAM events, cluster & topology state changes being logged)
4. Verifies the driver acquires the new primary and is able to run queries/commands

Also, a version of the above with the driver constantly doing reads & writes would be helpful for validating that the driver does survive cluster elections/Atlas upgrades.

summary: Driver does not properly fall back to IPv4 if IPv6 is not available
description: https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-ruby-driver/pull/873

summary: Separate monitoring and non-monitoring auth mechanism negotiation paths
description:
Currently the non-monitoring connection looks at server features to figure out what authentication mechanism to use, when it has just received an ismaster response with all relevant information filled out. Additionally there is a test that mocks the local feature inspection and therefore only handles half of the actual logic, plus it doesn't handle 4.0+ mechanism negotiation at all.

Make the non-monitoring connection make all of its decisions based on the ismaster response it received and ignore what is in the server features. Add another test that non-monitoring connection's ismaster response does not change server features (those get updated through SDAM paths). Redo the auth mechanism negotiation test to handle all auth cases (CR on old servers, SHA1 on pre-4.0 servers and SHA256 on 4.0 with mechanism negotiation).

summary: Create a topology base class
description: In order to implement 1442 there needs to be a base class for topologies to hold fields common to all of them, like the server addresses.

summary: Document random server selection matching staleness requirements
description:
The spec requires a random server within staleness window to be selected. Currently the driver selects the first server.

This touches the same code as server description compatibility check.

summary: Correctly handle monitoring when duplicating clients
description:
Currently when a client is duplicated (via #dup or #with), monitoring is sometimes copied into the new client and sometimes a brand new monitoring is created. In the former case event subscriptions in the new client affect the old client, in the latter case event subscriptions in the old client are not inherited.

Let's have the event subscriptions be copied so that the new client has subscribers to the original client but changes to the new client do not affect the original client.

summary: Eliminate address storage in topologies
description:
Currently the single topology stores its seed address, which is subsequently used for deciding whether a host should be added to a cluster. Other topologies do not store any addresses.

The address storage is confusing because the address stored is a seed address rather than the current address (for example, seed address may be missing the port number).

As part of spec compliance as well as improved diagnostics, topology should take the list of addresses off its cluster. Topologies are tied to clusters hence this does not create additional dependencies. This change also makes topologies more sensible in that their data reflects their current state.

We still need to store the seeds, mostly for transitioning from unknown topology to other topologies, and this job will be handled by cluster for the time being.

summary: Move handling logic from events to cluster
description: Currently SDAM logic is split between cluster, event handler classes and topology classes. This makes it hard to follow and, since the changes ultimately must be made on the cluster object, having some of the logic elsewhere makes the code violate encapsulation and have awkward patterns. This ticket is for moving SDAM logic from event handler classes to the cluster.

summary: Document monitoring option
description: This is not documented on Monitoring where it's used, and the docs on Client where it's given can be improved.

summary: Optionally wait for background threads to terminate
description:
Currently there is no way to wait for background threads to terminate. This most notably creates races in SDAM tests which manually run inspectors on the topology while said background threads could also possibly be running the inspectors.

On a related note, the stop? call returns true if the thread is killed or suspended, and should be replaced with alive? calls to indicate whether a thread is killed.

summary: Do not publish heartbeat events when monitoring is disabled
description: Since other SDAM events are not published when monitoring is disabled, heartbeat events should also not be published.

summary: Test interaction between sdam_proc & with/dup/clone
Add missing checkIfHasPrimary invocations
description: Looks like we are not invoking checkIfHasPrimary upon a me mismatch, and we should audit the other instances where the spec requires it be invoked.
